{{ doc.company }}, with address at 1200 H MCY Bldg. Edsa cor. Seminary Road Quezon City hereinafter referred to as â€œEmployerâ€.

{{ doc.applicant_name }}, with address at {{ job_app.address }} referred to as â€œEmployeeâ€ .

Hereinafter together referred to as â€œthe partiesâ€

WHEREAS:

A. Employee currently occupies the position of {{ doc. designation }} - {{ job_opening.location }} Employer and has been in the service of the Employer since {{ frappe.utils.format_date(doc.offer_date, "MMM. dd, yyyy") }}
B. In the course of Employeeâ€™s employment and the performance of his duties in the service of the Employer, certain confidential information may be disclosed by the Employer to the Employee;
C. Consequently, the parties have agreed to enter into this Confidentiality and Non-Compete Agreement in respect of such information disclosed.

NOW IT IS HEREBY AGREED as follows:

In this AGREEMENT, unless the context otherwise requires or expressly provides, the following words shall have the following meanings respectively:

1. â€œConfidential Information â€œmeans any information disclosed by Employer or its Personnel to the Employee, whether disclosed orally, in writing or to any other medium relating to computer systems, programs, software, tests, data, trade secrets, prototypes, demonstrations, drawings, samples, devices, specifications, materials of whatever description, copyrights, patents know how and other proprietary or intellectual property rights of the Employer;

â€œPersonnelâ€ means any shareholder, director, consultant, officer, employee, servant or other personnel, legal advisers or accounting advisers of Employer.

â€œSpecific Purposeâ€ means any business discussions, correspondence, or negotiations held between the parties for evaluating a business transaction in the course of the Employeeâ€™s service to the Employer, and any other purpose agreed upon in writing by the parties.

â€œEmployerâ€ means the party who engaged the services of the Employee and who furnishes Confidential Information to the Employee in the course of the latterâ€™s service to the Employer;

â€œEmployeeâ€ means the party in the service of the Employer who receives Confidential Information from the Employer.

In this agreement, reference to the plural shall include the singular and vice versa.

2. The Employee acknowledges and agrees that the Confidential Information, including any Confidential Information disclosed by the Employer or its Personnel to the Employee is confidential and propriety to the Employer, that the Employee has no proprietary interest therein whatsoever and that the Confidential Information shall be treated as set out below.

3. Due to disclosure of Confidential Information to the Employee in the course of his service to the Employer, the Employee hereby irrevocably undertakes that he shall, and shall use all reasonable endeavors to keep all Confidential Information in his possession and hold the Confidential Information in the confidence and in particular shall:

(i) Not use any of the Confidential Information in any way other than for the Specific Purpose;
(ii) Not disclose the Confidential Information to any third party without the prior written consent of the Employer except as required by law;
(iii) Take such precautions and make such arrangements as are reasonably necessary to protect the confidential Information.
(iv) Not make or have made any copy, record or duplication of any of the Confidential Information disclosed or reduce it to writing or in any medium, if disclosed orally, except as is reasonably necessary for the Specific Purpose and as expressly approved in writing by the Employer.

4. Subject to Clause 10 and as may be provided in any other agreements between the parties in relation to the Specific Purpose, the Parties obligations in Clause 3 shall survive any termination of their rights to use the Confidential Information or any party of it for the Specific Purpose and withdraw of the whole or any part of the Confidential Information from the Employeeâ€™s possession by the Employer shall not affect the Employeeâ€™s said obligations.

5. The Employeeâ€™s obligations in relation to the whole or any part of the Confidential Information shall not apply or shall cease to apply ( as the case may be) in accordance with Clause 6, if (i) the whole or the part of the Confidential Information is publicly available or otherwise in the public domain at the time of disclosure to the Employer or (ii) subsequently becomes publicly known other than through the act or default of the Employee; or (iii) the whole or the part of the Confidential Information was already in the possession of the Employee at the time of disclosure and such was not acquired directly from the employer.

6. The Employer and /or Employerâ€™s Principal shall not retain ownership of any drawings designs, specifications, manuals, photographs, reports, and documentation transmitted to the Employee during the course of the employment and the Employer and/ or Employerâ€™s Principal shall also retain the rights of all patentable inventions and registered designs contained within the information disclosed. At the Employerâ€™s request the Employee shall immediately deliver or cause to be delivered to the Employer and at the Employerâ€™s cost:
(a) All papers, documentation, tapes, photographic material, computer storage, media and other material containing Confidential Information or any specified part of it supplied to Employee in the course of his employment with the Employer together with any copies or extracts thereof or computerized record or record in writing or otherwise of orally transmitted Confidential Information, which have been made (whether or not in breach of this Agreement);
(b) Any Prototypes, models, samples and devices forming part of the Confidential Information.

7. Employee hereby agrees that he will not, either on his own account or in conjunction with or on behalf of any person to the detriment of the Employer or its Personnel (or any successors thereto), carry on or be engaged, concerned or interested, directly or indirectly, in carrying on a business that is indirect or direct competition with business of the Employer, except to the extent any such business engages in such competition as of the date hereof or as otherwise approved or expressly consented to majority of all the members of the Board of Directors of the Employer. Employer shall immediately notify the Board of Directors of the Employer. Employer shall immediately notify the Board of Directors in writing or any potential conflict or interest which they may be involved in upon discovery of such conflict.

8. The restriction provided in Clause 7 above all apply to all parties to this Agreement beginning the date execution of this Agreement and shall terminate five (5) years after the separation of employment from the Employer, for any cause.

9. No relaxation, forbearance or delay by the Employer in enforcing any of terms of this Agreement shall prejudice, affect or restrict the employerâ€™s rights or constitute a waiver of such rights or of breach (unless the Employer waives a breach or a right in writing), nor shall waiver by the Employer of any breach or right operate as a waiver of a subsequent of continuing breach thereof.

10. In the event that the Employee commits a material breach of any term, condition or of this Agreement, the Employer shall have the right to terminate the services of the Employee and / or seek recovery for damages. The employee acknowledges that in even of breach of this Agreement, damages may be an inadequate means of redressing any loss damage suffered by the Employer. The Employer in its sole discretion is entitled entitled to seek any injunctive or other equitable form of relief it deems necessary.

11. The restrictions contained in this Agreement are considered to be reasonable by the parties but in the event that any such restriction shall be found to be void but would be valid if some part of them were deleted or the period or area of application reduced such restriction shall apply with such modifications as may be necessary to make it valid and effective.

12. In the event that any of the provisions of this Agreement shall for any reason be held to be invalid, illegal or unenforceable, the remaining provisions of this Agreement shall be unimpaired.

13. This Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the Republic of the Philippines and the parties hereby submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the counts of the Republic of the Philippines.

14. This Agreement shall survive the termination of the employment of the Employee with the Employer and continue in full force and effect after unless terminated with the mutual Consent of the parties.

15. This Agreement may be executed by any one or more of the parties hereto in nay number of counterparts each of which shall be deemed to be an original, but all such counterparts shall together constitute one and the same instrument.

{% set job_applicant = doc.applicant_name %} {% set job_app = frappe.get_doc("Job Applicant", doc.job_applicant) %} {% set job_opening = frappe.get_doc("Job Opening", job_applicant.job_title) %}
{{ doc.company | upper }}
PROBATIONARY EMPLOYMENT AGREEMENT

{{ doc.applicant_name | upper }}
{{ job_app.address }}

Dear Mr/Ms. {{ doc.applicant_name | upper }},

Since you have successfully passed a physical and medical examination proving that you are free from any contagious diseases and other ailments, and that you are physically and medically qualified for the job you have applied for, and for having submitted the required clearances, {{ doc.company }} is employing you as a probationary employee, with the designation of {{ doc.designation }} - {{ job_opening.location }} under the following terms and conditions:

1. This probationary employment is for a period not to exceed six (6) months commencing on {{ frappe.utils.format_date(doc.offer_date, "MMM. dd, yyyy") }} notify you in writing on or before the said date that your services will be continued and transferred to the permanent payroll, or for any other reasons. During the said six months period, you will be working with us on a trial basis so that we may determine your qualifications and suitability for the job. Your conversion to regular status, in particular, shall be determined on the basis of satisfactory performance i.e., determined by your dependability, efficiency, initiative, attitude toward work, the company, its officers and other colleagues, cooperation, judgment, punctuality, quality/quantity of work, educability, craftsmanship, and professionalism, in accordance with the standards prescribed by the Company for the regularization of your employment.

2. The Company reserves the right to terminate, without prior notice and for any just cause, your services at any time during your probationary period. In this event, you shall be only entitled to collect only your wages up to the end of working hours of the last day of actual service.

Just to emphasize, your failure to meet any of the companyâ€™s standards is a ground for termination of your probationary employment even prior to the expiration of the period provided herein. Your probationary employment may also be terminated prior to the expiration of the stipulated period for just or authorized causes and for such other grounds as may be authorized by law and by the Companyâ€™s policies, rules and regulations, including but not limited to misrepresentation or concealment of material facts at the time you applied for employment with the Company.

3. As an {{ doc.designation }} - {{ job_opening.location }} you will be required to report for work in {{ job_opening.location }} unless assigned to other place of work.

{% for row in doc.offer_terms %} {% if row.offer_term == "Basic Salary" %}

4. For giving entire time and attention to the work assigned to you during the working hours of the Company, you shall be paid a salary of {{ row.value }} , payable semi - monthly. Such compensation shall be subject to withholding tax as well as deductions for contribution to SSS, Pag-ibig, and Philhealth, and such other deductions as prescribed by law. Further, the Company shall have the right, and for the avoidance of doubt, you hereby authorize the Company to offset, deduct or withhold from your compensation or any benefits which accrued in your favor any unpaid obligations to the Company

{% endif %} {% endfor %}

5. By working with the Company, you agree among others, to abide by all the Companyâ€™s rules and regulations which it shall be your duty and know immediately upon hiring; and, to refrain during your employment with the Company, from working for another employer whether within or outside your working hours with the Company, without the prior written consent of the Company.

6. By working with the Company, you agree to abide by the established working hours of this Company, which is for a period of Monday to Saturday (8) hours per day, subject to the Companyâ€™s established shifting schedules. Beyond the 8-hour period, it is subject to an overtime pay.

7. Pursuant to the policy of â€œno work no payâ€, you hereby waive any right to compensation for time lost from the established working hours. Further, non-compliance with working period shall be subject to the Companyâ€™s administrative proceeding for disciplinary action. For purposes of avoidance of any doubt, you shall not perform any overtime work on weekday or on Sundays or legal holidays, unless specifically directed by your supervisor. Your Immediate Superior is {{ doc.employee_name }} - {{ doc.employee_position }} , unless you are assigned to another supervisor of which you will be duly notified.


8. The Company shall not be responsible for any expenses you may incur in the execution of the duties unless you obtain prior consent and approval of the Management for such necessary expenses.


9. You agree that all records and documents of the Company, and all information pertaining to its business or affairs or that of its affiliated companies are confidential, and that no unauthorized disclosure, reproduction, or taking out of the same be made by you at any time during or after your employment in the Company.


10. You agree that all Company records, documents and assets in your possession, custody or control shall immediately be surrendered to your supervisor if requested during the employment period; and the termination thereof, whether requested or not. This includes the turn-over of passwords of all your computer files, retrieval of which will be a pre-requisite for the processing of your clearance and the release of your remaining salaries/wages and/or benefits.


11. This probationary employment does not entitle you to vacation leave and/or sick leave, or to any other benefit that is or my hereafter be granted to regular and permanent employees of the Company; EXCEPT those provided herein and such other benefits as may be granted under the law.


12. You agree that in case you resign from the Company, you shall not apply with any competitor or with any company with the same line of business as with the Company for at least two (2) years from the effectivity of date of resignation.


13. You also agree that should you go abroad for reason of employment, you will abide by the all stipulations stated above. In particular, violation of Clauses 9, 10 and 12 of this contract will subject you to civil action, and you hereby agree to pay damages to the Company, including but not limited to cost of suit.


14. It is expressly agreed and understood that there are no verbal agreements or understandings between you and the Company or any of its agents and representatives affecting this Agreement, and that no alteration or variations of the terms hereof shall be binding upon either party to this Agreement unless the same are reduced in writing and signed by you and the Company.


15. You agree to submit to personal search before leaving the Company premises and submit a lie-detector test if asked to.


We welcome you to our organization and trust that your association with us will be mutually beneficial.


Your signature in the space provided hereunder signifies your acceptance of the foregoing.



Very truly yours,

{{ doc.company | upper }}

MARIBETH A. HALLARE
HRAD Manager 		RYAN T. CHUA
VP-Sales

CONFORME:

I hereby acknowledge receipt of the original of this contract, and hereby unconditionally agree to all the terms stated herein. This further acknowledges that I have been duly informed of and have understood the standards that I am required to meet in order to become a regular employee of the Company.

__________________________
{{ doc.applicant_name | upper }}
__________________________

Instructions:
  1. The purpose of this appraised form is to provide a written record for the staff and his superior on their respective performance. Superiors are urged to be frank in their evaluations both for the benefit of the employee and the accuracy of the appraisal record.
  2. Key Result Areas and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) are set based on the agreed and approved KRAs and KPIs for the next 12 months, upon employment or Review Cycle.
  3. KPIs should be directly linked to the overall Corporate Business KRAs/KPIs. Action plans must be specific, measurable, attainable, realistic & time-bound (SMART).
  4. Each KRA will also consider the Job Profile Competencies created by each business unit.
  5. KRA and KPI are quantitative and measurable goals, critical in meeting job objectives. Indicate results achieved and if not achieved, explain why.
Guide Rating Definitions:
EXCEPTIONAL (EP) 5 Significantly exceeded performance criteria, targeted Key Performance Indicators (KPI), Service Level Agreement (SLA) by 150% and above.
ABOVE TARGET (AT) 4 Exceeded performance criteria, targeted Key Performance Indicators (KPI), Service Level Agreement (SLA) by 101%-149%
ON TARGET (OT) 3 Has achieved performance criteria, targeted Key Performance Indicators (KPI), Service Level Agreement (SLA) by 95%-100%
BELOW TARGET (BT) 2 Has met performance criteria, targeted KPI, and SLA by 70%-94%
UNACCEPTABLE (UP) 1 Has met performance criteria, targeted KPI, and SLA by 69% and below.
